steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole. The underlined sentence begins with "However," which signals a contrast, and it introduces Villasana's second subject category: ordinary people, illustrated by a young girl on a sidewalk.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To emphasize that Villasana prefers to focus on famous figures in her work.': The underlined sentence actually moves away from famous figures (cultural icons) toward ordinary people, so this directly contradicts what the sentence does. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To offer an overview of Villasana’s artistic style.': The sentence gives one specific example of a subject type, not a broad overview of Villasana's technique or methods. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To demonstrate that Villasana collaborates frequently with other artists.': Nothing in the underlined sentence mentions collaboration. This introduces an idea not present in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'To provide an example of an everyday individual that Villasana portrayed in her work.': The sentence explicitly introduces an ordinary person (a young girl on a sidewalk) as a concrete example of Villasana's second subject category. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
